pi_items.source: Official Sygic documentation (https://www.manula.com/manuals/sygic/sygic-gps-navigation/1/en/topic/view-amp-units) confirms 'Map font size' is a real setting in the View & Units section

Task description references changing from 'Default' to 'Large', but specific enumeration of font size options not found in official documentation. The APP package references font_large in skin configuration, suggesting 'Large' is likely a valid option.

Sygic support documentation and developer resources confirm the actual settings file is 'settings2.dat', a binary file stored in the app's private data directory (Android/data/com.sygic.aura/files/Android/), not a SharedPreferences XML file. The export_result.sh script incorrectly looks for XML files in /data/data/com.sygic.aura/shared_prefs/ which is not where Sygic stores settings.

privileged_info_summary: The task targets the real 'Map font size' setting in Sygic GPS Navigation, located in the View & Units menu as documented in official Sygic documentation. However, the metadata claim about the settings file pattern is incorrect. Sygic GPS stores settings in a custom binary file called 'settings2.dat' located in Android/data/com.sygic.aura/files/Android/, not in the standard Android SharedPreferences XML format or location (com.sygic.aura_preferences.xml in /data/data/com.sygic.aura/shared_prefs/). The export_result.sh script will not find the settings in the location it's checking, creating a flaw in the task's verification logic.
